assumptions: The developer has a supported browser, an email address, and a local environment able to send HTTP requests.
No GoCardless sandbox account or access token exists yet.
Sandbox payer test bank details are used; live money movement is out of scope.

developer_goal: Create a GoCardless sandbox account and complete the earliest official first test payment authorisation path.

first_success.outcome: Unresolved among Collect-payments Billing Request authorisation, Send-payments payouts setup, and partner OAuth integrations.
first_success.observable_signal: Send your first API request demonstrates a completed sandbox Billing Request and a new Customers dashboard entry, but Set up also presents Send payments and later partner guides.

route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete sandbox route only after Collect versus Send versus partner surface is fixed; do not invent a winner across unequal product goals.

candidates.name: Sandbox Collect payments via Billing Request Flow (BACS example)
candidates.reason: Official getting-started API request guide walks this path to a dashboard customer, but Set up presents Collect and Send as peer API functions and libraries versus raw HTTP remain tied.

candidates.name: Sandbox Send payments / payouts first success
candidates.reason: Set up lists Send payments as a primary API function with a different success definition from mandate authorisation.

candidates.name: Partner OAuth integrations
candidates.reason: Send your first API request links a partner getting-started path that connects to users' GoCardless accounts instead of a single merchant sandbox token.

required_path.action: Sign up for a free GoCardless sandbox testing account.

required_fields.label: Terms of service and related agreements

required_path.observable_result: The developer can open the sandbox dashboard at manage-sandbox.gocardless.com.

required_path.action: Create a Read-Write sandbox access token from Developers and copy it.
required_path.observable_result: A sandbox access token is available once for API calls.

required_path.action: Create a Billing Request and Billing Request Flow for the selected scheme, then open the hosted authorisation URL.

required_fields.label: Mandate scheme (example docs use bacs)
required_fields.notes: Scheme choice may vary by country and product; docs demonstrate bacs.

required_path.observable_result: An authorisation_url is returned for the hosted payer flow.

required_path.action: Complete the sandbox payer authorisation using documented test bank details and confirm the customer appears in the dashboard.
required_path.observable_result: Customers in the sandbox dashboard shows the newly created customer.

prerequisites.description: A developer environment capable of calling the GoCardless sandbox API is required.

external_gates.description: Partner integrations require OAuth connection to users' GoCardless accounts instead of only a merchant sandbox token.

unavoidable_waits.description: Hosted payer flow completion is required before the customer appears for Billing Request success.

platform_outcomes.description: GoCardless creates Billing Request and Billing Request Flow resources and returns an authorisation_url.
After payer completion, a customer record is visible in the sandbox dashboard.

excluded.item: Installing a specific official client library language tab
excluded.reason: Set up allows direct HTTP as an equal alternative to libraries; choosing one language would invent a tie-break.

excluded.item: Create a payment after first authorisation
excluded.reason: Send your first API request lists Create a payment as a next step after the customer is visible.

excluded.item: Live environment tokens and real payments
excluded.reason: Getting started confines the guide to Sandbox.

uncertainties.question: Should Collect-payments Billing Request authorisation, Send-payments, or partner OAuth define GoCardless first success?
uncertainties.impact: These are peer product goals with different tokens, objects, and observable terminals, so lexicographic selection cannot invent a winner.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus policy choice of one getting-started goal, then verification that its current sandbox docs form a single complete path.

uncertainties.question: If Collect payments is selected, which mandate scheme and request style (library language versus raw HTTP) are canonical?
uncertainties.impact: Scheme and client-library tabs can change prerequisites and request details without a documented single default.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official recommendation of one scheme and one request surface for the shortest first authorisation path.
